governing_requirement: Bind every consumed bridge artifact to actual encoded bytes and canonical decoded pixels plus media, owner, ontology, provider/model/workflow/runtime, target/protected authority, QA, certificate, lineage, transform, and signed revocation evidence; missing, malformed, ambiguous, or drifted evidence must prevent consumption and cache reuse.

existing_enforcement.evidence: Frozen v1 request/receipt schemas require source encoded/decoded identities, decoder/orientation/color semantics, still/video scope, canonical subject/assignment, target/protected artifacts, provider stack, output artifacts, transform report, QA, authority, lineage, eligibility, trust, signature, and canonical payload hashes.

validate_bridge_exchange binds direct request IDs and payload hash, media scope, source declaration, subject, release/capability IDs, transform, target/protected lineage, one output per intent, output owner, Mode A package lineage, resource limits, certificate, and production release/capability documents.

Receipt validation recomputes artifact_identity_sha256 from encoded/decoded/content/owner/dimension/transform semantics and binds each output to exact source decoded pixels, transform, intent, and lineage.

Certified receipts require the exact supplied operational certificate, output/source/subject/release/coordinate scope equality, active authority, fresh revocation observation, QA pass, and transform round-trip.

operational_certificate issuance independently reads governed source/output files and recomputes encoded and decoded hashes before signing, but this byte-reading gate is not exposed as a general bridge-consumption/cache decision.

negative_probes.probe: request ontology SHA changed and request re-signed while the receipt remained bound to the original request payload
negative_probes.interpretation: post-request tampering is detected by the request payload binding

negative_probes.probe: receipt provider model-artifact SHA changed and receipt re-signed without updating provider stack SHA
negative_probes.interpretation: provider stack identity is asserted but not canonically recomputed from model/workflow/runtime components

negative_probes.probe: inspect positive receipt for an exact cache identity/freshness binding
negative_probes.interpretation: the v1 receipt has no cache binding, so exact cache reuse cannot yet be authorized or invalidated by one deterministic decision

missing_proof: No bridge-consumption gate reads the actual source and output files under a governed root and recomputes encoded bytes, canonical decoded pixels/masks, decoder/orientation/color/value semantics, size, and content summary at use time.

Provider stack_sha256 and execution_fingerprint_sha256 are not independently recomputed from model artifacts, workflow, runtime, environment locks, selected route, and execution observation; the provider-component drift probe remains validator-clean after a trusted re-sign.

The request payload hash detects mutation, but no explicit compatibility comparison proves the requested API, package format, ontology version/hash, and release/capability facts equal the supplied adopted production documents.
QA report and selection-evidence hashes are asserted without byte-resolving their governed evidence artifacts during bridge consumption.
No exact cache identity binds release, capability, request, source pixels, media scope, ontology, policy, provider bundle, artifacts, transform, QA, certificate, lineage, and revocation position.
No cache-freshness decision proves certificate/release/provider/ontology/policy/revocation currency immediately before reuse, or produces a typed tombstone/no-reuse result on drift.

Current document validation does not itself prove safe-root path resolution, actual byte equality, or cache invalidation for every consumed output; those checks must be one fail-closed producer decision rather than caller convention.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement a producer-owned, actual-byte-verifying artifact consumption and cache-reuse decision over the frozen v1 exchange after MF-P6-09.01 and MF-P6-09.02 are adopted.
implementation_packet.path_relationship_to_held_work: Exact allowed files are disjoint from held 08.05/08.06/08.07/09.01 paths and the prepared 09.02 transform files. Frozen v1 schemas, validation.py, operational_certificate.py, fixtures, and bridge/__init__.py are read-only candidates.
acceptance_criteria: Validate the complete request/receipt/certificate/release/capability transaction before resolving any artifact path.
Resolve every source/output/QA/selection evidence path beneath an explicit governed root and reject traversal, absolute, reparse, hardlink, symlink, special-file, case-alias, and missing-file states.
Read actual encoded bytes and canonical decoded pixels/masks and recompute all declared hashes, dimensions, decoder/orientation/color/alpha/dtype/value/content semantics.
Recompute provider stack and execution fingerprints from exact model, workflow, runtime, lock, selected-route, and execution components; asserted hashes alone cannot pass.
Prove requested API, package format, ontology version/hash, release, capability, and bridge contract equal the supplied adopted production documents.

Bind every output to exact media scope, canonical owner, intent, target/protected authority, executable transform evidence, QA gate vector, certificate scope, parent lineage, and signed revocation position.
Derive one canonical cache identity from release, capability, request, source/media, ontology, policy, provider, artifact, transform, QA, certificate, lineage, and revocation hashes.

Cache reuse requires exact cache identity plus current unrevoked certificate/release/provider/ontology/policy state and a declared freshness bound; drift returns a typed no-consume/tombstone decision.
Decision record binds policy ID/hash, exact normalized evidence, cache key, freshness, allow/reject result, deterministic reasons, and canonical record hash.
Missing, malformed, ambiguous, substituted, stale, revoked, or drifted evidence fails closed and never silently falls back to a weaker/wrong/empty artifact.
Frozen v1 contracts, semantic profile, fixtures, catalog, and all held worker paths remain byte-unchanged.

observed_verification.command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'all_positive_documents or request_ownership_transform or receipt_authority_parent'
observed_verification.scope: existing structural transaction, ownership/transform, and authority-lineage checks

observed_verification.command: in-memory trusted re-sign probes for request ontology drift, provider component drift, and receipt cache presence
